equal_policy.custom_body: ((r1 is Ok) == (r2 is Ok)) && post1_self_.block_size == post2_self_.block_size && post1_self_.start_addr == post2_self_.start_addr && post1_self_.end_addr == post2_self_.end_addr && post1_self_.allocated_addrs.len() == post2_self_.allocated_addrs.len() && post1_self_.free_addrs.len() == post2_self_.free_addrs.len()
equal_policy.rationale: The spec lets the allocator pick any address from old free_addrs, so the specific chosen address (and the resulting allocated_addrs/free_addrs sets, the Bitmap's internal usage/next_free/bits, and the raw data_addr/end_addr pointers) is implementation-defined. The invariant block_size/start_addr/end_addr must match, and exactly one address moves from free to allocated on Ok (none on Err), which is captured by comparing set cardinalities plus matching Ok/Err variant.
